title: ESCALATE: Client _maybe_fetch_quota embedded in shell script (untestable)

text: {"type":"escalate","finding":"Client _maybe_fetch_quota embedded in shell script","severity":"important","description":"The _maybe_fetch_quota function is 26 lines of Python embedded in the scripts/tmux-claude-status bash/python polyglot script. It cannot be imported or tested directly. Validation tests re-implement the function to verify its logic. Drift between the test re-implementation and the actual script is a maintenance risk.","options":[{"label":"Option 1: Extract to scripts/tmux_status_client.py","solution":"Move function to a standalone .py file importable by both the polyglot script and tests.","pros":"Direct testing, no drift risk, clean separation.","cons":"Changes deployment model — extracted module needs to be importable. Changes script architecture."},{"label":"Option 2: Keep embedded, add source-hash verification","solution":"Tests compute a hash of the function source in the script and compare to expected hash.","pros":"No structural change.","cons":"Fragile — breaks on whitespace changes. Still not testing the real code."},{"label":"Option 3: Accept re-implementation approach","solution":"Keep current test approach. Function is stable and small (26 lines).","pros":"No structural change, no maintenance burden. Drift risk is low for a small stable function.","cons":"If someone changes the script function but not the test, tests verify stale logic."}],"recommendation":"Option 3 for now. Function is stable, small, and drift risk is manageable. Extract in a future iteration if the function grows."}
{"type":"user-decision","approach":"Test harness extracts the Python code from the polyglot bash/python script and tests it directly. No structural change to the script itself."}
